2990.name: Inside the Lighthouse
2990.description: The stone walls match much of the outside's wear and tear. The air is cold and crisp from a slight breeze seemingly coming down from above. A spiral staircase offers a way to ascend the lighthouse, and a wooden door leads out of the building.

2991.name: Lighthouse - 2nd Floor
2991.description: The stone walls holding up the spiral staircase are so cold you can feel it permeating just by being nearby. The spiral staircase descends both upwards to an opening above to a platform and downwards to the lighthouse's bottom floor. A gentle breeze smelling of salt and brine flows down into the lighthouse from above, and the sound of crashing waves can be heard coming from above as well.

1593.name: Beneath the Pier
1593.description: Waves crash against several large wooden pillars that extend down below the surface of the water supporting a platform above. A wooden ladder is built into the side from a platform where smaller vessels may be docked. Seagulls squawk overhead and strut along the sides of the pier above you expectantly.

1606.name: The Preparation Chamber
1606.description: The vestibule is lined with racks with various arms and armor. None very impressive, but they're better than nothing. A couple stone benches circle the center of the room around a central iron brazier lit with a fire. A large lattice gate to the west leads into the arena. A ramp to the south leads up and out of the chamber.

2968.name: Fishing Hut
2968.description: Made entirely out of koa wood, this hut is decorated with rods, tackle and bait all strewn about haphazardly. You feel the sudden urge to pick up a fishing hobby just gazing at the sheer amount of fishing goods found here. A counter in the corner of the room encases the more exotic baits and tackle.

npcs.name: Boras
npcs.description: Boras doesn't seem to care how his age and line of work has affected his appearance. He reeks all the way from where you stand of fish and lack of

2971.name: Within the Enormous Mahogany Tree
2971.description: The tree ascends for tens of feet above the nearest building as it towers over the city. The branches sway from the salty breeze sweeping in from the south. Clambering back down will require significant skill.

npcs.name: Komura
npcs.description: The shifty elf is perched on one of the branches overseeing the city. He appears quite focused on whatever it is he is doing, keeping his attention

2992.name: Lighthouse - 3rd Floor
2992.description: The surrounding walls are made up of glass and open windows. The breeze from the outside sweeps in strongly to the opposite window and down the opening to the floor below. The platform is made up of sturde koa wood and at the center stands a large, bright beacon that rotates clockwise. Faint, ominous whispers can be heard from all around you.

npcs.name: Zekle
npcs.description: This extremely aged darkling is adorned in all black clothing and displays a wicked look as he peers about the room. The man's eyes glow a deep red ominously as he chants in an ancient language.

3021.name: Among the Trees
3021.description: Large branches extend up and outwards in all directions here, offering plenty of ways to gain footing. The clanging of steel from below disrupts the natural susurration of the leaves. Down leads to a sandy path with heavy foot traffic.

npcs.name: Viserra
npcs.description: Trained by the legendary Finrodin in the forests of Evermore, Viserra was one of his star students.  She has brought those skills to bear in service to

3030.name: Inside the Cave
3030.description: The cave opens up to a massive cavern. Darkness shrouds every direction of travel except for the way out. Sound carries off to a seemingly endless distance. It is difficult to determine if walking in any direction would be a good idea.

npcs.name: Abeloth
npcs.description: This enormous dragon of deep black obsidian towers over you with a terrifying gaze. Its belly glows a crimson red as it prepares to envelop you

1594.name: The Ornamented Pier
1594.description: Kulani spared no expense right out of the gate, or pier in this case. The boards beneath your feet are made from mahogany and polished to withstand the punishment of heavy traffic. Upon inspection, the railings on the perimeter of the landing on either side of you are made entirely of platinum poles engraved with precious gemstones. North leads to more mahogany boards lined with gaudy railings leading to the shore.

npcs.name: Flamboyant ticketmaster
npcs.description: A flamboyant ticketmaster stands here with a wickedly pompous smile. His task doubles as the welcome master of the island and ticket salesman for those

1595.name: The Boardwalk
1595.description: Twinkling sands glisten in the sunlight beneath the boardwalk on either side of you. Benches line the sides offering quick refuge behind the rails to travelers as they would either wait for their ferry or gather their bearings before entering the city. The pier extends to the south where ships may be docked. To the north the boardwalk leads to the sandy shores of Kulani.

1596.name: On the Pier
1596.description: The pier extends a ways out to the ocean where boats may be docked. The base of the pier offers steps up to the mahogany platform, sand is strewn about the steps kicked up from the beach. Platinum railings on either side of you offer a steady ascent or descent. The south leads out to the dock while the beach leads in all directions.

1597.name: Path to Kulani
1597.description: The sandy path leading from the beach narrows into a trail covered by the shade of koa and mahogany trees. Seagulls and other various birds can be seen flapping from limb to limb overhead. The trail continues north, while south leads to the beaches of Kulani.

1598.name: Kulani's Royal Archway
1598.description: Before you stands a large stone archway with chiseled divots that spiral upwards. Smooth stone walls extend to the east and west encasing the city. The stone path widens passed the arch to the north, while the cobblestone trail narrows to the south, ending at the beach.

1599.name: Southern Streets of Kulani
1599.description: The path is accompanied by towering flagpoles with waving flags of all walks of life. Flags representing Mareldja, Crown on the Water, is accompanied alongside the Ramluf Cadain dwarves, the Decaren Jorel explorers, the Barcus entrepreneurs and even a beatifully embroidered flag of Isvaelin waves proudly among them. An overwhelming sense of diversified unification stands as a testament to the future between these long-forgotten allegiances. The streets lead north to the town center, and south to a massive arch. A smaller stone path leads east, and a building with enticing beach gear is to the west.

npcs.name: City guard
npcs.description: Clad in grey and a deep shade of turquoise, the guard walks the walls of Mareldja with a slow, measured tread.

npcs.name: Flamboyant ticketmaster
npcs.description: A flamboyant ticketmaster stands here with a wickedly pompous smile. His task doubles as the welcome master of the island and ticket salesman for those

npcs.name: Kulani citizen
npcs.description: carries an air of vain pompousness as she glares at all the tourists. How rude.

1600.name: Ryley's Supply Shop
1600.description: This building is packed with aisles of mahogany display tables showcasing everything one might need for a quick visit to the beach, or an extended stay on the island. The walls are completely covered by shelves with an assortment of hand-made trinkets, beach ornaments and other knickknacks that might appeal to someone. Beach gear is exhibited by the windows at the front of the shop to entice passing tourists to make a day trip to the beach. The shop exits to the east.

1601.name: Southern Streets of Kulani
1601.description: The cobblestone path is adorned with tall columned mahogany flower pots with drooping ferns. Tents of various shapes, sizes and colors line the path while the hoots and hollars of sales pitches can be heard in all directions. Why not pick up a souvenir? The cobblestone path continues south to a massive arch, and north leads to the town center. A stone building decorated with window boxes stands to the west. To the east, a lattice fence covered in ivy runs parallel to the street.

1602.name: Kulani Town Center
1602.description: A crossroad of streets extend in all directions here. In the center sits a massive ornate fountain which protrudes water from its center showering down to its lower tiers. The sound of flowing water muffles the banter of passersby. South offers a route to the shore accompanied by decorated flagpoles adorned with gold lacings that spiral upwards. Immediately to the east lies the shopping bazaar designed to attract tourists. West offers more accommodations, while the path leading north is bustling with heavily armed guards trampling the cobblestone path.

npcs.name: City guard
npcs.description: Clad in grey and a deep shade of turquoise, the guard walks the walls of Mareldja with a slow, measured tread.

1603.name: Western Streets of Kulani
1603.description: The cobblestone path is lined with mahogany flower boxes against the northern and southern buildings displaying a beautiful assortment of plumeria and small hibiscus flower bushes. Tall marble poles also line the sides of the street for other seasonal decorative purposes. The cobblestone path leads east and west, while two businesses can be found to the north and south.

1604.name: Western Streets of Kulani
1604.description: The stone architecture is prominent here more than ever. A large building stands to your south with multiple windows, signifying some sort of inn. An enormous arch lines the path to a massive circular stone structure to the west. The path to the north decends to a plateau, while east leads to the town center.

1605.name: Before the Arena
1605.description: Stunning! A large, oval stadium comprised of sand is encompassed by stage seating all around it. A wooden booth stands on the opposite end of the stadium overlooking the arena. A sign is displayed on a small wooden kiosk by the entrance to the stadium. North descends into the arena, while south leads to the western streets of the city.

npcs.name: Master of ceremonies
npcs.description: This overdressed man is very excitable, able to hype up most everyone for the spectacle of the games. He not only sells tickets, but sells the idea of the arena itself. The master of ceremonies makes an excellent salesman.

2963.name: On the Beach
2963.description: With the pier off to your west, the ocean waves gently swell in from the south. Tiny sea crabs scuttle about and dig into the sand as the waves knock them around. The sun cascades over the glistening sands, offering a warm pleasant feeling of perfect beach weather. Why not kick off your shoes and enjoy the sand? The beach continues to the east and west here.

2964.name: On the Beach
2964.description: Walking along the beach you notice a few beachgoers set up with blankets and umbrellas, enjoying the weather. Children are playing in the water and making various sand castles. The water swells gently up to your feet, tempting you for a quick dip in the ocean water. What's stopping you? The beach continues east and west here.

npcs.name: Muscular bodybuilder
npcs.description: This man is pure muscle. He struts around the beach like his sole purpose for building muscle was all for this moment.

2965.name: On the Beach
2965.description: The sands glisten all around you, what a sight! The ocean swells in from the south as you witness children splashing around in the ocean water. Blankets and beach chairs are strewn about as far as the eye can see. A trail away from the beach leads north, and the beach continues to the east and west here.

2966.name: On the Beach
2966.description: The beach population grows sparse this far out, but a small hut stands before you made entirely out of koa wood. Heavy foot traffic disturbs the otherwise pristine layer of sand throughout the rest of the beach. The beach continues east and west here.

2967.name: Eastern Shore
2967.description: The shore bends to the north here as gentle waves creep in the from the east. The harmonic rhythm of the ocean waves are almost hypnotizing, beckoning you to take a few moments to relax and enjoy the scenery. The beach extends far to the west and continues north here.

2969.name: Park Path
2969.description: The cobblestone path narrows and is accompanied by tall, koa wood planters with bright floral arrangements within them. Young koa and mahogany trees are planted periodically along the path on either side of you. The southern walls of Kulani are met with koa wood lattice fencing which encase the park. The fence itself is covered in beautifully green ivy that weaves itself around the entire perimeter. The path continues to the east and splits off to the north. A large, native mahogany tree sits in the center of the park.

2970.name: Before an Enormous Mahogany Tree
2970.description: This aged mahogany tree was preserved probably because of how massive it is. The tree offers shade that extends in all directions for yards. As materialistic as the island's inhabitants appear to be, it's hard to believe they kept this beauty around. All directions lead away from the tree.

2972.name: Park Path
2972.description: Young koa and mahogany trees accompany potted ferns and floral arrangements as the cobblestone path circles around the park. The lattice fence corners here extending to the east and south. A massive mahogany tree stands at the park center to the southeast.

2973.name: Liani's Flowers and Fruits
2973.description: The sweet auroma of flowers lingers in the air here as rows of potted flowers sit on the tables before you. Tiered display crates of fruits line the western wall consisting of various sweet fruits; apples, grapes and mangos being among them. You can see that everything is well stocked and immediately recovered to keep the display tables as attractive as possible. The shop exits to the east.

2974.name: Kulani Bazaar District
2974.description: The sudden increase in the noise level of toursits and haggling signifies that you are in the bazaar streets of Kulani. Tents are erected on either side of the cobblestone path, while street performers perform various acts to entertain the tourists during their shopping experience. Seemingly dozens of conversations are happening at once. Tents of all shapes and sizes are strewn about, leading further east. To the west stands a tall fountain displayed at the town center.

2975.name: Plain White Tent
2975.description: Stepping into the tent, you are presented with countless gems, rocks and unbroken geodes. Perhaps you should try your luck and buy one. All minerals, even those nonindigenous to the island, are found here. You never know what you might get! The lighting in the tent is oriented in such a way that it gives sparkle to everything being displayed; it's almost hypnotizing. South leads to the streets of the bazaar.

npcs.name: City guard
npcs.description: Clad in grey and a deep shade of turquoise, the guard walks the walls of Mareldja with a slow, measured tread.

2976.name: Black Silken Tent
2976.description: The absence of light within this tent is noticeable. Contrary to the other tents of the bazaar, this one carries a sense of gloom and despair. A pungent odor coming from within the tent wafts by you to the outside. Searching for the cause, you find potions, vials and beakers meticulously-aligned on the display tables within the tent. It smells awful. North leads to the streets of the bazaar.

npcs.name: Nolan
npcs.description: The mysterious man grins at you as he arranges his variety of stock. He skillfully tosses flasks and potions around with incredible precision. You could swear he's using some kind of telekinesis.

2977.name: Kulani Bazaar District
2977.description: Bazaar tents lead all the way up to the eastern walls of Kulani here, even attaching to them. Between the incessant banter of haggling, children whining and the performance of street musicians, it would be claustrophobic to some people. The east leads under an arch exiting the city, while west leads to more shopping.

2978.name: Golden Laced White Tent
2978.description: Inside the tent you find mahogany tables aligned into aisles with a path down the middle. On top of the tables are a plethora of gem encrusted items. Goblets, knives, sandals... if it exists, this vendor has found a way to put an expensive price tag on it. It seems to be working, as the tent is overcrowded with nobles and other flamboyant tourists. Finding something actually useful might be too few and far between. South leads to the streets of the bazaar.

npcs.name: Clufus
npcs.description: The man sports a pair of jewelers lenses just above his brow. He bounces between assisting customers and his workbench where he tinkers with gemstones and clothing, eager to tailor them together for added value.

2979.name: Blue Silken Tent
2979.description: This tent appears to be strictly dedicated to clothing and fashionable accessories. Suits, robes, dresses, hats and footwear are all found here in a very organized and segregated manner. Whether you need a sunhat for the beach, or hiking boots for rough exploration, this is the place to be. North leads to the steets of the bazaar.

2980.name: Northern Streets of Kulani
2980.description: The change in tone is immediately noticable. There are less tourists, and more armed mercenaries occupying the cobblestone path here. Your presence goes noticed as well-equipped men size you up as they pass by. South leads to the town center, while north is cluttered with more soldiers preparing for, or retreating from a battle. To the east and west are stone buildings that offer basic good for the everyday traveler.

2981.name: Laccy's Delicacies
2981.description: This business operates more like a restaurant, as many of its customers choose to sit down, relax, and enjoy the tasty treats Laccy has for sale here. Koa wood tables and chairs are placed along the perimeter of the establishment, with only a few unoccupied seats available. Everyone indulging seems to be enjoying themselves. Why not join them? East leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

2982.name: Edrig's General Goods
2982.description: While the recent events may have dwindled some of his stock, the owner does his best to compensate and recover the shelves. Everyday items ranging from liquid containers, rations, candles and other perishables can be found for a bargain here. West leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

2983.name: Northern Streets of Kulani
2983.description: The noise level grows louder here as a mixture of men shouting orders is combined with the cries of the wounded. You begin to notice spots of blood at your feet leading east into a makeshift hospital. The clanging of steel from the smithery to the west adds to the commotion causing your sense of hearing to overload from the constant barrage of noise. The cobblestone path stretches north to a massive arch, and south to the town center.

npcs.name: Acting Captain Gonnan
npcs.description: Acting Captain Gonnan stands here vigilantly surveying the scene, occasionally dishing out orders to his "troops". His flashy appearance stands out amongst

2984.name: The Kulani Smithery
2984.description: The clanging of steel from multiple anvils being hammered upon barrages your ears as you enter. Forges burn brightly from being constantly billowed, and the sharpening wheels are in constant motion as the master smith and his apprentices attempt to keep up with the demand. This building probably wasn't designed for this sort of heavy operation. The heat of the forges mixed with the odor of sweat makes your stay here quite unpleasant. The doorway to the east leads out to the commotion on the cobblestone streets.

2986.name: Before the Archway
2986.description: A large stone archway offers entrance to the streets of Kulani to the west. To the south, a narrow dirt path can be found; perhaps an old fisherman's trail. Stone walls extend to the north and south which are immediately concealed in thick layers of ferns and underbrush. The cobblestone path extends east and west here.

2987.name: Path to the Eastern Cliffs
2987.description: The path is surrounded by dense ferns and koa trees to the north and south, blocking vision in either direction. The fresh smell of salt and brine flows in from the east accompanied by the sound of crashing waves. The strong ocean breeze through the brush and trees create a very tranquil psithurism. Kulani's large archway can be seen to the west.

2988.name: Overgrown Path
2988.description: The overgrown path is surrounded by dense ferns and a thick layer of foliage. The air seems calm as the scent of salt and brine sweeps in from the east. A vague indication of broken branches lead to the east.

2989.name: Before the Lighthouse
2989.description: Before you stands a tall lighthouse overlooking the eastern shore protruding on a rocky outlook. Oddly, the tower seems a bit worn and unkept from the outside. A wooden door offers an entrance to the tower while west leads to an overgrown makeshift pathway.

2993.name: Royal Bank of Kulani
2993.description: The interior design does not match the outer structure's design at all. The flooring is entirely made up of marble. Koa wood counters stretch from east to west, wall to wall, with ropes attached to platinum poles extending from each window. Best pick a line now if you want service for your banking needs. South leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

npcs.name: Eli
npcs.description: The glasses on this gnome make his eyes look twice as big as normal. He strokes his elegantly long moustache awaiting your inquiry with a blank stare.

2994.name: Alon's Outfitters
2994.description: Standing before the counter, you notice a small anvil and forge in one corner while a sharpening wheel sits alongside an elongated mahogany table with various sewing and stitching supplies. A sign hangs on the east wall. North leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

2995.name: Kulani Inn
2995.description: The Kulani Inn lives up to its reputation as one of the most desirable resort destinations as you are greeted with carpeted flooring above a marble foundation. A large mahogany reception desk sits in the center of the room offering patrons of the island refuge for their stay. The lobby continues south where a visible intricate staircase can be seen, while the north leads out of the inn.

2996.name: Kulani Suites - 1st Floor
2996.description: Extravagant! A red carpet lines the floor trailing east to west where the inn offers rooms to its patrons. The staircase before you, made entirely out of immaculately cut marble, leads upwards to the second floor. The hallway continues east and west here, leading to the suites on the first floor.

2997.name: Suite Facilities
2997.description: Marble flooring covers the ground here with lattice paneling that lines the walls with various ivy plants coating them. The room has a very pleasant aroma of blooming jasmine. Two doors against the south wall lead to locker rooms separated by gender; southwest for men, southeast for women. North leads to the Inn's vestibule.

2998.name: Kulani Suites - 2nd Floor
2998.description: The marble flooring is lined with a red carpet that stretches east to west while a railing to the north overlooks the inn's lobby below. The staircase leads down to the first floor of the inn. East and west offer rooms to the second floor of the inn.

2999.name: Kulani Suites - 2nd Floor
2999.description: The carpeted western hallway ends with three doors leading into rooms of the inn. Mahogany doors surround you in all directions except to the east, where the second floor's balcony overlooks the lobby.

3000.name: Suite 202
3000.description: This room is very large. It stretches from north to south with windows that give the tenant a view of the streets and the beach resepectively. Two large beds sit against the eastern wall with dressers and clothes cabinets along the western wall. This room appears to be occupied by several adults, as suggested by the two beds, and by how completely filled the cabinets and dressers are. The room exits to the west.

3001.name: Kulani Suites - 2nd Floor
3001.description: The carpeted eastern hallway ends with three doors leading into rooms of the inn. Mahogany doors surround you in all directions except to the west, where the second floor's balcony overlooks the lobby.

3002.name: Women's Locker Room
3002.description: Two rows of lockers line the eastern side of the room while the western wall bears the washroom facilities for the Inn's female patrons. The marble flooring is clean and well kept. A fresh, flowery scent subdues any other foul odors that may come from a room such as this. Northwest leads out of the locker room while south leads further in to the bathing facilities.

3003.name: Suite 206
3003.description: This room offers a view that overlooks the streets of Kulani, and a peek at the northern side of the island. Covered by a layer of trees, only the open sea can be seen. A bed sits against the eastern wall of the room, and a couple dressers occupy the western wall. The belongings within the room suggest it is occupied by a noblewoman, as heels and gaudy sandals are aligned by the door, and colorful sundresses and skirts are hung within the clothing cabinet. The room exits to the south.

npcs.name: Winry
npcs.description: The hooded elf in green robes paces around the room in quiet haste. She affixes several holy trinkets around the room to bolster good omens and energy. As quickly as she moves around, she is unnaturally quiet.

3004.name: The Pub
3004.description: A long, polished mahogany bar extends from north to south at the far side of the room with an insane amount of liquor variety shelved behind it. Tables and barstools are nearly filled as the tourists and locals congregate together sharing stories of their adventures or the latest gossip. You can spot one available table and a couple barstools next to some cackling patrons. The room exits to the east, leading back the the inn's lobby.

3005.name: Before the Archway
3005.description: You stand before a massive arch that leads towards the largest structure in the city. A large garden accompanies the path to the west with various tropical flowers on either side. West leads passed the arch to the large stone structure, and east brings you to the western streets of Kulani. Along the wall, south leads to another structure with iron bars over the windows.

3006.name: Courtyard
3006.description: The massive circular stone structure before you stands tall atop a flight of stairs. The top of the building is domed with stained glass windows surrounding it. A grass lawn covers the area on either side of the cobblestone path with scattered dandelions swaying in the breeze. The courtyard carries a pleasant scent of flora as you bask in the scenery. The stairs lead west to the foot of the building, and east brings you under the arch.

3007.name: Before the Council Chamber
3007.description: Easily three stories tall, the chamber stands as a significant landmark for the island. Several grooved stone pillars support an arched awning leading to the entrance of the chamber. West leads into the chamber passed several rows of pillars. Looking east, you can see most of the city leading up to the eastern arched entrance to Kulani.

npcs.name: Palace guard
npcs.description: A guard of the palace stands here guarding the entrance to the council chambers. You might want to think of a good reason for being here if you intend

3008.name: Inside the Chamber
3008.description: Within the chamber, the marble flooring foundation lies beneath a fancy red carpet that leads in all directions. Along the northern and southern walls, sconces holding tall candles burn incessantly. Impressive scenic paintings hang between the candle lighting, continuing to the west. The carpet branches to the north and south, leading to polished mahogany doors.

3009.name: Director's Office
3009.description: A massive table stands in the center of the room here. Against the northern wall is a large desk consisting of several drawers with gold plated handles. Dozens of scrolls and books sitting atop the table are opened up displaying information about Kulani legislation and trade protocol. The walls are decorated with portrait paintings of high-class individuals, presumably of various nobles of Kulani. South leads out of the office to the main chamber.

npcs.name: Edwin
npcs.description: Edwin carries himself elegantly as he moves about. His nose always seems dug into some kind of book, scroll or parchment. You notice a faint shimmer of a glowing white aura expelling from his robe.

3010.name: The Gallery
3010.description: Various paintings line the west wall on either side of a pair of large, mahogany doors and continuing to the north and south. The paintings consist of a wide range of landscapes; mountain foothills, river valleys, snowy tundras and beach sunsets depict an array of artistic visions. Red carpeting stretches in all directions here.

npcs.name: Palace guard
npcs.description: A guard of the palace stands here guarding the entrance to the council chambers. You might want to think of a good reason for being here if you intend

3011.name: Council Meeting Room
3011.description: This large room hosts an enormous mahogany table with chairs pushed in all around it. A large map of the island can be seen against the far wall, displaying all of the native terrain and constructs. A few books and scrolls rest on top of the table, as well as some leftover bowls and plates from a recent meal. Paintings of landscapes and portraits line the walls between lit candle sconces, giving the room a pleasant setting. The room exits to the east.

npcs.name: Lionel
npcs.description: A tall masculine man, adorned in gaudy clothing of fine silks commands

3012.name: The Gallery
3012.description: This southern hallway displays many landscape paintings, some of which look rather familiar to you. One depicting a dense forests behind a massive cityscape accompanied by a large body of water in the foreground appears quite like Mareldja. The gallery of paintings covering the walls bend to the north and west here. A sweet aroma a well-cooked food escapes from the seams of the door to the south.

3013.name: The Kitchen
3013.description: A sweet smell cooked meat and seared vegetables fills your nostrils as you enter. Stoves line the western wall separated by a deep sink in the middle made of marble. A massive table stands in the middle of the room for staging the food and other various preparations. A large windowed metal door to the south is cold to the touch, storing perishable goods, while east leads to more kitchen space. The kitchen exits to the north.

npcs.name: Head chef
npcs.description: The chef, a lanky ogre covered with greasy hair and reeking of elf livers,

3014.name: The Kitchen
3014.description: The north wall of the kitchen is lined with various kitchen amenities. Bread proofers, herb boxes, knife and spice racks... everything one might need to begin preparations for a large meal. A massive table stands in the middle of the room covered in flour and herbs. A windowed metal door to the south glows with radiant warmth, smelling heavenly of freshly baked dough. The kitchen continues to the west.

3015.name: Library
3015.description: Polished mahogany shelves cover the walls all around the room. The center of the room consists of shorter shelves standing waist-high and placed into aisles. Cushioned leather seating is offered along the northern wall on either side of the entrance. A wealth of information can certainly be found here.

3016.name: Before the Archway
3016.description: The massive stone arch is accompanied by walls that stretch east and west around the perimeter of the city. Men, elves and dwarves are intermixed among the crowd of soldiers. Finding a way under the arch might prove difficult as a continuous flow of soldiers flow in and out of the city. Men can be spotted standing guard at the base of the arch, while elves find perch on top of the walls with bows drawn. North leads out of the city into more commotion, while south offers a more appealing escape from the bustle of war.

3017.name: Path to the Northern Shore
3017.description: The pathway isn't ideal for this kind of traffic. The tall koa trees are accompanied by an extremely thick layer of ferns and shrubs at their base, forcing people to squeeze by one another, sometimes knocking each other over through all the commotion and urgency. The path leads north to more chaos, and south towards a massive archway.

3018.name: Path to the Northern Shore
3018.description: No time to enjoy the scenery unless you want to risk getting knocked over. Armed men and women push their way to the north as a few retreat south. The wounds on some of these deserters look particularly gruesome. Spats of blood coat the sands, giving off a sparkling red hue. The bustle of traffic continues to the north and south here.

3019.name: Path to the Northern Shore
3019.description: The trees and ferns here appear scratched or otherwise battered down as the heavy foot traffic tramples the sandy pathway. The verbal commotion grows louder to the north, continuing with the chaos. South leads against the flow of traffic and eventually towards Kulani's northern archway.

3020.name: Path to the Northern Shore
3020.description: The clanging of steel immediately fills your ears as a battle ensues before you; it's almost deafening. The once glittering sands of Kulani is now being spattered with blood at your feet leading down to the beach. Mahogany and koa trees line the path on either side of you. Glancing into the trees, you can barely make out humanoid figures with bows drawn between the swaying outer branches. A mixutre of men passing you by head north into battle, while wounded men retreat south into the city for aid. The leadership here seems very unorganized. A gnarled plant with twisted roots grows here, its leaves dark and waxy. You have lost 3000 experience.

3022.name: Path to the Northern Shore
3022.description: The sandy path is congested with heavy foot traffic as armed men and women trample the ground here. The sands display a red hue from blood that has been kicked around. The mahogany and koa trees end to the north where the beach can be seen. South leads towards the northern arch of Kulani, and ferns congest the base of the trees on either side of you.

3023.name: Off the Beaten Path
3023.description: Getting through here is a squeeze. Tall, dense ferns surround you in every direction. Indications of bent or otherwise broken branches are signs that this path has been made by something before you. The disturbed brush leads west while east opens up into a sandy path.

3024.name: Shadowy Trail
3024.description: The loose trail of broken branches is accompanied by a deep darkness. The tall brush overshadows you in the middle of a dense forest of koa trees, making it impossible to see the sky. A thick layer of foliage at your feet make it hard to gain any confident footing. The indications of broken branches lead east and west here.

3025.name: Shadowy Trail
3025.description: Not only is it unnaturally dark here, it's quiet. Except for you. Every step you take is disproportionately loud with the cracking of twigs and crunching of dead leaves. The only thing spookier than the overwhelming silence is the occasional beastly cackle and movement of something large in the brush somewhere in the distance. The indications of broken branches lead east and west here.

3026.name: Shadowy Trail
3026.description: Making your way through the dense ferns is a challenge. Every step is met with the shuffling of smaller critters nearby that scatter from your position. Going unnoticed would prove to be a difficult task for even the stealthiest of rogues. Just pray you don't attract the wrong attention with your clumsy movement along the trail. The indications of broken branches lead east and west here.

3027.name: Shadowy Trail
3027.description: A tiny bit of light from the sky above manages to shine through, but not enough to help you navigate in any way. The cramped passage between the thick growth of ferns seem to open up slightly to the northwest. To the east, broken branches indicate a narrow path of travel.

3028.name: Shadowy Trail
3028.description: The brush around you sways slightly with a gentle, yet unpredictable breeze. The path opens up to the north, accompanied by the slight howl of something massively hollow in response to the breeze. A cramped trail of dense ferns are slightly beaten to the southeast, indicating a narrow path of travel, if you push hard enough.

3029.name: Cave Entrance
3029.description: Tall, green ferns waist high coat the ground in all directions. It is so quiet here that you can hear the droplets of water landing on the ferns from where the cave overhangs the entrance. The padded down foliage around you indicate that this area has not been used by man, but by something larger. Much larger. The dismal entrance of the cave offers you no amount of comfort as to what may lie within. Pushing back through the ferns seems like the sensible choice.
